title: Add instructions for updating the vendor in e.g. podman
body:
Maybe I'm missing something obvious but: How do i use `go mod replace` to test out local changes to this library with the `go.podman.io` alias in the middle?

```
$ diff --git i/go.mod w/go.mod
index 9dc4189d43..7ac74ebc2b 100644
--- i/go.mod
+++ w/go.mod

@@ -191,3 +192,5 @@ require (
        gopkg.in/tomb.v1 v1.0.0-20141024135613-dd632973f1e7 // indirect
        tags.cncf.io/container-device-interface/specs-go v1.0.0 // indirect
 )
+
+replace github.com/containers/storage => ../container-libs/storage
$ make podman
CGO_ENABLED=1 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build \
	 \
	-ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v5/libpod/define.gitCommit=43c95d2c0bdfc71d005e015fe93b3e7a48f39adf-dirty -X github.com/containers/podman/v5/libpod/define.buildInfo=1760380947  -X github.com/containers/podman/v5/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v5/libpod/config._etcDir=/etc -X github.com/containers/podman/v5/pkg/systemd/quadlet._binDir=/usr/local/bin -X github.com/containers/common/pkg/config.additionalHelperBinariesDir= ' \
	-tags "grpcnotrace   libsqlite3 systemd libsubid  seccomp " \
	-o bin/podman ./cmd/podman
package github.com/containers/podman/v5/cmd/podman
	imports github.com/containers/common/pkg/ssh
	imports github.com/containers/common/pkg/config
	imports github.com/containers/storage/pkg/lockfile
	vendor/github.com/containers/storage/pkg/lockfile/lockfile.go:10:2: use of internal package go.podman.io/storage/internal/rawfilelock not allowed
```

It compiles if I override [go.podman.io/storage...without](http://go.podman.io/storage...without) pulling any of my local changes,.

comments.body: It seems to work fine in e.g. https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27251/files#diff-33ef32bf6c23acb95f5902d7097b7a1d5128ca061167ec0716715b0b9eeaa5f6 , that looks ~natural to me. What is the confusing part?

OK first, it's in-retrospect but wasn't immediately to me obvious that I needed to override all three of the libraries here to point to the local build.

So going with
```
diff --git i/go.mod w/go.mod
index 9dc4189d43..982d677b53 100644
...
+replace go.podman.io/storage => ../container-libs/storage
+replace go.podman.io/image/v5 => ../container-libs/image
+replace go.podman.io/common => ../container-libs/common
```

OK...but running `go mod vendor` seems to just be a no-op? I think this might be because all the local import paths are still using `github.com/containers/storage` and it things only work because of a DNS-level alias for network-based vendoring?

https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING_GO.md#testing-changes-in-a-dependent-repository

It is already document I would say, the paragraph below also shows the local replace.

I guess we could explain it a bit more that one generally want to replace all at once generally.


> OK...but running go mod vendor seems to just be a no-op? I think this might be because all the local import paths are still using github.com/containers/storage and it things only work because of a DNS-level alias for network-based vendoring?

How are the local import paths github.com/containers/storage?  If you still are using an old podman code base that doesn't use the go.podman.io/* import paths then replaces won't work for it of course. You need to use the latest code which was converted to the new import paths.
Yes sorry, the problem was I *thought* I was on a recent podman commit but actually wasn't.
